PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-08-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

This matter was put on for hearing with no appearance required for the purpose of admitting the will. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The will dated MAY 10, 2019 is admitted to probate. M. David DeSantis is appointed as the Executor of the estate with limited authority. Bond is not required. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for September 07, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Vedica Puri. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Feb-08-2021 Continued To Mar-08-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David DeSantis appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that the fourth amended petition referenced an attachment, that it does not see any documents attached, that it would like to see a death certificate for Robert C. Elking and that it is interested in knowing whether or not there is a death certificate. Mr. DeSantis stated that he would have to order a death certificate, that he does not know how long it will take to receive one, that it will be coming from Michigan, and apologized to the Court stating that the family is giving bits and pieces of information. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to March 8, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-20-2021 Continued To Feb-08-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-09-2020 Continued To Jan-20-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David DeSantis appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that there is some reservation with respect to whether there was a formal adoption by the Decedent's step-father, that if the Decedent was not formally adopted, there may be heirs through his biological father and that this raises the issue of whether the paternal heirs are entitled to notice. Mr. DeSantis stated that he was only able to locate a name change in 1986, that he confirmed with the Husband of the living Aunt that there was no adoption, that the Husband was the family historian and that he has dates of death for all of the four grandparents. Mr. DeSantis also stated that there was one maternal Aunt who died on August 17, 2006, that the only living relative that existed was Norma E. Hall and that this is consistent with a statement from the Decedent to him. The Court stated that it appears the paternal heirs would be entitled to notice and that it would like to see more. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to January 20, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: D. Johnson. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-02-2020 Continued To Dec-09-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
